title: Spinel Stone; prices, colors, spinel vs ruby comparison & more!
description:
Spinel Gemstone Guide: Colors, Quality, Prices & What to Avoid!
Spinel is one of the most underrated but coveted natural gemstones in the jewelry industry. Often mistaken for ruby or sapphire, spinel stands out for its vivid colors, exceptional brilliance, and durability. In this video, we take a deep dive into everything you need to know about natural spinel gemstones — from their color ranges (red, pink, blue, lavender, black, and more) to their origin, price per carat, and gemstone quality factors.

If you are looking to purchase a spinel or to inquire for a custom jewel or engagement ring, do send me an email at info@fineandflux.com
You can also view my custom jewelry portfolio here:
https://fineanflux.com/pages/custom-jewelry-gallery2

We'll explore:
-What is spinel? (Natural vs. synthetic spinel)
-How to tell the difference between spinel and ruby
-Popular spinel colors and their meaning
-The best spinel for engagement rings or everyday jewelry
-Why jewelers love spinel for its luster, hardness (7.5–8 Mohs), and durability
-Spinel gemstone price comparisons vs. sapphire, ruby, and black diamonds
-Common treatments and how to spot spinel imitations
-Where spinel gemstones are found (Myanmar, Sri Lanka, Vietnam, Tanzania)
-Spinel’s symbolism, metaphysical properties, and birthstone meaning (August birthstone)

We'll also compare spinel vs sapphire, spinel vs ruby, and other gemstones of similar hues to help you choose the perfect one for your custom jewelry or engagement ring.
Whether you're a gem enthusiast, collector, or shopping for a unique engagement ring, this video will give you everything you need to know about spinel gemstone value, beauty, and durability.

Montreal, Canada 45.501689-73.567256

#jewelry #spinel #gemstones #jewelry #jewelrymaking 
Fine and Flux Jewelry is a jewelry brand that specializes in custom design and engagement rings. We create all our jewelry in-house, in a homely studio downtown Montreal, Canada, free shipping worldwide!
We create all our jewelry videos to entertain and educate you so you have a better understanding on how it's made, how to make an educated purchase, and also what to look out for.
Do not try anything you see in these videos at home.
For more information or to book an online appointment please write to us at info@fineandflux.com and we will be happy to get back to you quickly.

00:31 Spinel stone colors

05:11 Spinel in comparison to rubies and sapphires
11:55 Spinel prices
19:15 Origins
20:22 Synthetics & imitations
